name: workspace-formatter
displayName: Workspace Formatter & Command on All Files
description: Format all files in a workspace or folder, and apply any VS Code command to all matching files.

commands.title: Workspace Formatter: Format All Workspace Files
Workspace Formatter: Format All Files in Folder
Command on All Files: Apply on Workspace

items.patternErrorMessage: Each extension must begin with a period, e.g. ".ts"

commandOnAllFiles.includeFileExtensions.markdownDescription:
Only files whose extension appears in this list will be processed. Each entry **must include the leading period** — e.g. `[".html", ".css", ".js"]`. An empty list means no extension filter is applied (all files are eligible).

Ignored when `commandOnAllFiles.includeFiles` is set on the same command.

regex.description: A JavaScript regular expression searched against the file path in the form /workspaceName/relative/path/file.ext (separator: /).

flags.description: Optional RegExp flags. Only "i" (case-insensitive) is meaningful here.

commandOnAllFiles.includeFiles.markdownDescription:
List of regular expressions of **file paths to include**. When defined and non-empty this setting **overrides** `commandOnAllFiles.includeFileExtensions`.

Each entry has:
- `regex` — pattern tested against `/workspaceName/relative/path/file.ext`
- `flags` — optional RegExp flags (`i` is the only useful one)

Example:
```json
[
  { "regex": "/src/components.*\\.tsx" },
  { "regex": "/test/.*\\.py", "flags": "i" }
]
```

regex.description: A JavaScript regular expression searched against the file path in the form /workspaceName/relative/path/file.ext (separator: /).

flags.description: Optional RegExp flags. Only "i" (case-insensitive) is meaningful here.

commandOnAllFiles.excludeFiles.markdownDescription:
List of regular expressions of **file paths to exclude**. Applied after all include filters; a matching file is always skipped.

Each entry has:
- `regex` — pattern tested against `/workspaceName/relative/path/file.ext`
- `flags` — optional RegExp flags

Example — skip all generated files:
```json
[
  { "regex": "\\.generated\\." },
  { "regex": "/dist/" }
]
```

commandOnAllFiles.excludeFolders.markdownDescription:
Directory names to skip when scanning for files. The `.git` entry is **always** added automatically regardless of this setting, to prevent accidental corruption of your Source Control repository.

In a **Multi Root Workspace** you can also include a workspace folder's base name here to skip that entire root.

commandOnAllFiles.includeFolders.markdownDescription:
Glob patterns describing which **folders** to include. Only files whose parent directory satisfies at least one pattern will be processed.

There is no need to start a pattern with `**`. Including the path separator `/` prevents partial directory-name matches — e.g. `"/src/"` matches files inside a directory named exactly `src`, but not `src-test`.

When this list is empty or absent, all folders (except excluded ones) are eligible.

Example:
```json
["/src/", "/lib/"]
```

commandOnAllFiles.saveFiles.markdownDescription:
Controls what happens after a command is applied to a file.

- `true` *(default)*: Save the file if it was modified, then close its editor tab.
- `false`: Leave the file open in the editor with any unsaved changes.

command.description: The VS Code command ID to execute on each matching file. Find it via File > Preferences > Keyboard Shortcuts — locate the command and right-click > Copy Command ID.

includeFileExtensions.description: Override commandOnAllFiles.includeFileExtensions for this command only.

includeFiles.description: Override commandOnAllFiles.includeFiles for this command only.

excludeFiles.description: Override commandOnAllFiles.excludeFiles for this command only.

excludeFolders.description: Override commandOnAllFiles.excludeFolders for this command only. .git is always added.

includeFolders.description: Override commandOnAllFiles.includeFolders for this command only.

saveFiles.description: Override commandOnAllFiles.saveFiles for this command only.

label.description: Custom QuickPick label for this command (shown in the Command Palette picker). Defaults to the key name. Supports VS Code icon syntax, e.g. $(file-code) Format TypeScript.

description.description: QuickPick description (shown to the right of the label). Supports VS Code icon syntax.

detail.description: QuickPick detail line (shown below the label). Supports VS Code icon syntax.
